Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Michael Heavey Construction, Inc., a California corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Jean H. Hurrican, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Marin 1055 LP, a California limited partnership.
Alex Cartlidge, Plaintiff's Director of Finance of Operations, was placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Hurrican and Ms. Cartlidge appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Michael Heavey Construction, Inc., a California corporation.

Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Lease was filed on January 31, 2020.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and accompanying documents on defendant Michael Heavey Construction, Inc., a California corporation, occurred on February 3, 2020, and was effectuated on February 13, 2020.
Default was entered on March 5, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed September 18, 2020.

Plaintiff Marin 1055 LP, a California limited partnership, submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1: Standard Industrial/Commercial Multi-Tenant Lease dated 11/20/2018.........ID/EVID
Exhibit 2: Declaration of Alex Cartlidge filed 9/18/2020..............................ID/EVID

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Marin 1055 LP, a California limited partnership, and against the defaulted defendant Michael Heavey Construction, Inc., a California corporation, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $30,539.30.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,988.24.
Attorney Fees - $9,165.50.
Costs - $742.16.

Total - $42,435.20.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Plaintiff STIPULATES to the return of the exhibits.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up
